dimension: wallet-sdk-core per-chain raw-tx signing (Solana/Stacks/Stellar wallet providers + Bitcoin spoke service) and the skills/docs structure (granular SKILL.md + knowledge/features/*.md + docs/*.md) introduced by the Swaps API v2 PR.

files_read.role: Solana provider; PR added signAndSendTransaction(SolanaRawTransaction) + signTransaction/signAndSerializeTransaction/signTransactionWithAdapter/signTransactionWithKeypair helpers (+38 lines)
Stacks provider; PR added signAndSendTransaction(StacksRawTransaction) that deserializes the hex payload and routes through sendTransaction (+25 lines)
Stellar provider; PR added a Soroban rpc.Server, sendTransaction(XDR) broadcaster, and signAndSendTransaction(StellarRawTransaction) (+47 lines)
Stellar config types; PR added optional sorobanRpcUrl to both PrivateKey and BrowserExtension config variants (+6 lines)
Bitcoin spoke service; PR added signAndSubmitRawTransaction({rawTx,walletProvider,relayData?,accessToken?}) for the Bound 2-of-2 trading-wallet flow (+36 lines)
Re-export barrel; PR added deserializePayload, addressToString, PayloadType from @stacks/transactions
Defines SolanaRawTransaction {from,to,value,data} and adds optional signAndSendTransaction? to ISolanaWalletProvider
Defines StacksRawTransaction {payload,estimatedLength?} and adds optional signAndSendTransaction? to IStacksWalletProvider
Defines StellarRawTransaction; adds optional sendTransaction?(XDR) and signAndSendTransaction? to IStellarWalletProvider
RawTxReturnType union (8 chains, no Bitcoin) and TxReturnType<C,Raw> conditional mapper used to narrow the per-chain raw tx
Adds SwapsClientOptions { useBackendSubmitTx?: boolean } + swapsOptions? on SodaxOptionalConfig — the central backend-submit toggle
Adds 'backend' to SodaxFeature (error feature tag for BackendApiService/SwapsApiService)
NEW granular skill — single SKILL.md (no own knowledge subtree), links into parent integration/knowledge tree
NEW feature knowledge doc — full 21-endpoint client reference (methods, wire shapes, call shapes, status tables)
Parent broad skill — PR adds swaps-api row to the routing table and to the features reading list
Skills package map — PR adds sodax-sdk/swaps-api to the granular-skill inventory in 3 places
NEW human doc (169 lines) accompanying the feature; sibling to BACKEND_API.md/SWAPS.md
Frontend dispatcher: signAndBroadcastSwapsApiTx + isSignableSwapsApiChain + waitForTxFinality — per-chain sign+broadcast of the API raw tx
React mutation hook wrapping sodax.api.swaps.submitTx (retry:3)
React query hook polling sodax.api.swaps.getSubmitTxStatus every 1s until executed/failed
Orchestrator; PR added submitTx() (backend 2-step) + fallbackSwapSteps() (client relay) gated by useBackendSubmitTx (+310 lines)

key_findings: SIGNING COVERAGE MATRIX (which chains can sign+broadcast a Swaps-API raw tx, from apps/demo/.../signAndBroadcast.ts switch + isSignableSwapsApiChain): EVM, SUI, ICON, NEAR, SOLANA, STELLAR, STACKS, INJECTIVE are signable via the wallet-only dispatcher. BITCOIN is signable but NOT via the wallet dispatcher — it must go through BitcoinSpokeService.signAndSubmitRawTransaction (Bound 2-of-2 trading wallet). isSignableSwapsApiChain() returns true for the 8 wallet-dispatcher chains (Bitcoin excluded).

NEW per-chain methods added BY THIS PR (3 wallet providers + 1 spoke service): SOLANA, STACKS, STELLAR wallet providers each gained signAndSendTransaction(params: <Chain>RawTransaction); Bitcoin gained BitcoinSpokeService.signAndSubmitRawTransaction.

EXISTING/REUSED methods (NOT changed by PR — the dispatcher just calls them): EVM IEvmWalletProvider.sendTransaction(tx: EvmRawTransaction): Promise<Hex>; SUI ISuiWalletProvider.signAndExecuteTxn({ toJSON: async () => data }); ICON IIconWalletProvider.sendTransaction(tx: IcxCallTransaction); NEAR INearWalletProvider.signAndSubmitTxn(tx: NearRawTransaction); INJECTIVE IInjectiveWalletProvider.signAndSendTransaction(tx: InjectiveRawTransaction). The diff --stat confirms only solana/stacks/stellar provider files (+ stellar/types.ts) changed in wallet-sdk-core.

SOLANA signature: public async signAndSendTransaction(params: SolanaRawTransaction): Promise<TransactionSignature>. Body: const bytes = Buffer.from(params.data,'base64'); const versionedTx = VersionedTransaction.deserialize(bytes); const signedTx = await this.signAndSerializeTransaction(versionedTx); return this.sendTransaction(signedTx). Also added supporting public helpers: signTransaction(tx: VersionedTransaction): Promise<VersionedTransaction> (branches isAdapterMode → signTransactionWithAdapter else signTransactionWithKeypair); signAndSerializeTransaction(tx): Promise<SolanaSerializedTransaction> (signs then tx.serialize()); signTransactionWithAdapter(tx) (uses (this.wallet as WalletContextState).signTransaction, throws if missing); signTransactionWithKeypair(tx) (transaction.sign([keypairWallet]), throws in adapter mode). Imports SolanaRawTransaction added from @sodax/types.

STACKS signature: async signAndSendTransaction(params: StacksRawTransaction): Promise<string>. Body: const payload = deserializePayload(params.payload); if (payload.payloadType !== PayloadType.ContractCall) throw new Error('signAndSendTransaction: expected a contract-call payload.'); return this.sendTransaction({ contractAddress: addressToString(payload.contractAddress), contractName: payload.contractName.content, functionName: payload.functionName.content, functionArgs: payload.functionArgs, postConditionMode: PostConditionMode.Allow }). The raw tx's `payload` is the hex-serialized contract-call payload; sendTransaction signs+broadcasts in both private-key and browser-extension modes. New imports deserializePayload, addressToString, PayloadType added from @sodax/libs/stacks/core (which now re-exports them from @stacks/transactions).

STELLAR signatures (two new methods): public async sendTransaction(signedTx: XDR): Promise<string> — TransactionBuilder.fromXDR(signedTx, networkPassphrase) then this.sorobanServer.sendTransaction(tx); if response.status==='ERROR' throw StellarWalletError(...,'SEND_TX_ERROR'); return response.hash. AND public async signAndSendTransaction(params: StellarRawTransaction): Promise<string> — const signedXdr = await this.signTransaction(params.data); return this.sendTransaction(signedXdr). Errors use code 'SIGN_TX_ERROR' (sign) / 'SEND_TX_ERROR' (broadcast). Stellar now needs a Soroban RPC server: added private readonly sorobanServer: rpc.Server constructed from config.sorobanRpcUrl ?? STELLAR_SOROBAN_URLS[network] ({TESTNET:'https://soroban-testnet.stellar.org', PUBLIC:'https://rpc.ankr.com/stellar_soroban'}) with { allowHttp: true }. New imports: TransactionBuilder, rpc from @stellar/stellar-sdk; StellarRawTransaction from @sodax/types.

STELLAR types.ts change: added optional sorobanRpcUrl?: string ('Soroban RPC server URL used to broadcast transactions. Defaults per network.') to BOTH PrivateKeyStellarWalletConfig and BrowserExtensionStellarWalletConfig (alongside the existing rpcUrl which is documented as the Horizon URL).

BITCOIN signature (spoke-service, NOT a wallet provider): public async signAndSubmitRawTransaction(params: { rawTx: BitcoinRawTransaction; walletProvider: IBitcoinWalletProvider; relayData?: RelayExtraData; accessToken?: string }): Promise<string>. Guards: throws if this.walletMode !== 'TRADING'; throws if !rawTx?.data || !rawTx?.from (Bitcoin's API raw tx hits the permissive AnyRawTxSchema, so it is NOT schema-validated upstream — must guard manually). Body: const signedTx = await walletProvider.signTransaction(rawTx.data, false); const signedBase64Tx = normalizePsbtToBase64(signedTx); return this.radfi.requestRadfiSignature({ userAddress: rawTx.from, signedBase64Tx, relayData }, accessToken). accessToken defaults to this.radfi.accessToken. relayData ({address,payload}) is the relay identity from createIntent() and must be supplied by the caller (not recoverable from rawTx). New import: RelayExtraData from ../../types/relay-types.js.

INTERFACE pattern in @sodax/types: each new method is added to the wallet-provider interface as OPTIONAL (signAndSendTransaction?: (params: <Chain>RawTransaction) => Promise<...>) so existing provider implementations stay compatible. ISolanaWalletProvider.signAndSendTransaction? → Promise<TransactionSignature>; IStacksWalletProvider.signAndSendTransaction? → Promise<string>; IStellarWalletProvider gained BOTH sendTransaction?(signedTx: XDR)=>Promise<string> and signAndSendTransaction?(params)=>Promise<string>. The dispatcher null-checks the optional method and throws SwapsApiSignError(chainKey,'sdk-gap',...) when absent.

RAW TX TYPE SHAPES (packages/types/src/<chain>/<chain>.ts): SolanaRawTransaction = { from: SolanaBase58PublicKey; to: SolanaBase58PublicKey; value: bigint; data: Base64String }. StacksRawTransaction = { payload: string /*hex-encoded serialized tx payload*/; estimatedLength?: number }. SuiRawTransaction = { from: Hex; to: string; value: bigint; data: string /*Transaction#serialize() JSON, rebuild via Transaction.from()*/ }. EvmRawTransaction = { from: Address; to: Address; value: bigint; data: Hex }. (StellarRawTransaction at stellar.ts:90 has a `data: XDR` field consumed by signTransaction; BitcoinRawTransaction at bitcoin.ts:102 has `data` PSBT + `from`.)

RawTxReturnType union (common/common.ts:186) = EvmRawTransaction | SolanaRawTransaction | InjectiveRawTransaction | IconRawTransaction | SuiRawTransaction | StellarRawTransaction | StacksRawTransaction | NearRawTransaction — NOTE: BitcoinRawTransaction is intentionally NOT in this union. TxReturnType<C extends SpokeChainKey|ChainType, Raw extends boolean> is a conditional mapper that resolves a chain key to its per-chain return type and INCLUDES a BITCOIN branch (BitcoinReturnType<Raw>). The dispatcher narrows `tx as <Chain>RawTransaction` per chain-type branch.

FRONTEND DISPATCHER (apps/demo/src/components/swaps-api/lib/signAndBroadcast.ts) is the canonical client-side pattern, exporting THREE functions: (1) signAndBroadcastSwapsApiTx({chainKey,tx,walletProvider}): Promise<string> — switch on getXChainType(chainKey), narrows tx to the per-chain RawTxReturnType variant, calls the right provider method, returns spoke tx hash; throws SwapsApiSignError for sdk-gaps; (2) isSignableSwapsApiChain(chainKey): boolean; (3) waitForTxFinality(chainKey,walletProvider,txHash): Promise<void> — EVM/ICON waitForTransactionReceipt, SOLANA waitForConfirmation(txHash,'confirmed'), STELLAR waitForTransactionReceipt (poll Horizon after Soroban submit); SUI/NEAR/INJECTIVE need no extra wait. Custom error class SwapsApiSignError extends Error with (chainKey, reason:'sdk-gap'|'malformed-payload', message, payload?). This file documents that 'neither @sodax/dapp-kit nor @sodax/sdk ship a utility for this step — every consumer hand-rolls the per-chain dispatch' (a known SDK gap).

CENTRAL submit-tx + fallback mechanism: SwapsClientOptions { useBackendSubmitTx?: boolean } added to SodaxOptionalConfig.swapsOptions (sodax-config.ts). It is a CLIENT-SIDE runtime toggle (like logger), NOT part of backend-fetched config; distinct key so it never collides with the data `swaps` slot. Default false. SwapService got constructor param useBackendSubmitTx (stored readonly), and swap() branches: if this.useBackendSubmitTx → submitTx(params, created, deadline) (private; calls this.backendApi.swaps.submitTx(...) then polls this.backendApi.swaps.getSubmitTxStatus({txHash,srcChainKey}) until 'executed'); on ANY non-success (submit fail, terminal failed, abandoned, poll timeout) it falls back to fallbackSwapSteps(...) (the default client-side relay) so the swap still completes — safe because re-relay/re-post are idempotent. The single `deadline`/timeout is split between backend path and fallback (reserve up to a third, capped at 20s) so total wall-clock never exceeds one timeout.

dapp-kit submit-tx hooks: useSwapsApiSubmitTx (mutation, mutationKey ['swapsApi','submitTx'], retry:3, mutateAsync({request: SubmitTxRequestV2, apiConfig?})) wraps unwrapResult(sodax.api.swaps.submitTx(...)); useSwapsApiSubmitTxStatus (query, queryKey ['swapsApi','submitTx','status',txHash,srcChainKey], requires BOTH txHash AND srcChainKey via enabled gate, refetchInterval 1000ms stopping on 'executed'|'failed'). Note PR DELETED the old useBackendSubmitSwapTxStatus.ts and renamed the old hook into swapsApi/useSwapsApiSubmitTx.ts; all 22 swapsApi hooks live under packages/dapp-kit/src/hooks/swapsApi/.

SKILL.md ANATOMY (granular feature skill — swaps-api/SKILL.md, 62 lines, SINGLE file with no own knowledge subtree): YAML frontmatter with `name:` (kebab id e.g. sodax-sdk-swaps-api) and a long `description:` (one paragraph: what it is = class + access path + endpoint count, plus 'Use when …' trigger phrases, ending 'Skill links into the parent sodax-sdk knowledge tree.'). Body sections in order: H1 title; intro paragraph (access path, Result<T> never-throws contract, error tags); blockquote pointing to the higher-level orchestrator skill; '## Step 1 — Clarify with user before coding' (numbered clarifying questions); '## Integration workflow' (numbered list of relative links into ../integration/knowledge/ — ai-rules.md FIRST, then the feature md, then related feature mds, then result-and-errors/error-codes); a feature-specific anti-patterns sub-list; '## Migration workflow (v1 → v2)' (links to ../migration-v1-to-v2/knowledge/features/*); '## Verification' (numbered checks incl. `pnpm tsc --noEmit`); '## Related granular skills (same family)' (sibling skill links). Frontmatter in bridge/SKILL.md additionally shows `license: MIT` and `metadata: { version, author }` keys.

KNOWLEDGE FEATURE DOC ANATOMY (integration/knowledge/features/swaps-api.md): H1 '# <Feature> — `<ServiceClass>`'; intro (access path, never-throws + valibot-validation contract, error feature/context tags); '## Methods' (one big ```ts block listing every method signature grouped by area with comments); '## Wire shapes — bigint vs decimal strings'; '## Common call shapes' (sub-H3 per flow with code); '## Status fields' (markdown table of distinct status enums); '## Per-call overrides'; '## Custom endpoint'; '## Error handling' (Result branch example); '## Cross-references' (links to sibling feature docs + reference/error-codes.md).

DOCS FILE that accompanies the feature: packages/sdk/docs/SWAPS_API.md (NEW, 169 lines). It mirrors the knowledge feature md but is the human-facing SDK doc (sibling of BACKEND_API.md, SWAPS.md, BRIDGE.md, etc.). Sections: title; intro (access path); '## Methods' (ts block); '## Wire shapes'; '## Long-polling'; '## Examples'; '## Status fields' table; '## Configuration' (ApiConfig/BaseApiConfig/CustomApiConfig types); '## Result<T> and Error Handling'; '## See also' (links to BACKEND_API.md, SWAPS.md). PR also edited sibling docs BACKEND_API.md (-56), CONFIGURE_SDK.md, SWAPS.md (+8 cross-link). NOTE: docs/BRIDGE.md ALREADY EXISTS.

REGISTRATION TOUCHPOINTS for a new granular skill (3 edits, all small): (1) parent sodax-sdk/SKILL.md — add a routing-table row '| <Feature> API client (…) | [`./<feat>/SKILL.md`](./<feat>/SKILL.md) | "trigger phrases" |' AND append the feature md to the step-3 features reading list; (2) packages/skills/AGENTS.md — append `sodax-sdk/<feat>` to the granular-skills inventory in the package table, the consumer-situation table, and the directory-tree comment (3 spots); (3) add the feature md to integration/knowledge/features/ and (optionally) migration-v1-to-v2/knowledge/features/. The swaps-api skill REUSED swap.md + backend-api.md migration docs rather than adding its own migration feature file.

ERROR/CONFIG plumbing: SodaxFeature gained 'backend' tag (features.ts) — the HTTP-client layer (BackendApiService/SwapsApiService) errors carry feature:'backend', context.api:'backend', context.endpoint:<path>, cause:<transport failure>; this is distinct from domain features ('swap','bridge', …). errors/codes.ts changed +12 lines (new EXTERNAL_API_ERROR plumbing). @sodax/libs/stacks/core/index.ts is the only place a third-party (@stacks/transactions) symbol gets re-exported for provider use — the repo isolates third-party deps through packages/libs.
open_questions: Does a 'Bridge API v2' backend surface (analogous to /swaps/*) actually exist on the backend, or will the Bridge API reuse the same submit-tx/relay endpoints? The PR's submit-tx/fallback machinery (SwapService) is swap-specific; whether BridgeService needs its own backend-submit path or can share the swaps one is unverified.

BridgeService.bridge() returns TxHashPair { srcChainTxHash, dstChainTxHash } (per existing bridge/SKILL.md) — different from the Swaps create-intent → raw tx → submit-tx model. Need to confirm whether the Bridge API feature is a NEW typed HTTP client (mirroring SwapsApiService) or an extension of the existing on-chain BridgeService, because that changes which skill/doc to author.

The per-chain signAndSendTransaction methods are generic 'sign+broadcast an unsigned API raw tx' — they are NOT swap-specific, so Bridge raw txs of the same RawTxReturnType shape would reuse them as-is with ZERO new wallet-provider code. Confirm the Bridge API returns the same RawTxReturnType union (esp. whether it ever needs Bitcoin, which is excluded from RawTxReturnType and requires the Bound spoke-service path).

signAndBroadcastSwapsApiTx lives in apps/demo (a hand-rolled consumer helper), explicitly flagged as a missing shared SDK/dapp-kit utility. A Bridge equivalent would face the same gap — decide whether to promote a shared dispatcher into dapp-kit/sdk now or keep hand-rolling per feature.
replication_recipe: Per-chain signing for Bridge raw txs is ALREADY covered if the Bridge API returns the same RawTxReturnType variants — no new wallet-provider methods are required: reuse signAndSendTransaction (Solana/Stacks/Stellar/Injective), sendTransaction (EVM/ICON), signAndExecuteTxn (Sui), signAndSubmitTxn (NEAR), and BitcoinSpokeService.signAndSubmitRawTransaction (Bitcoin/Bound). Only add a new provider method if Bridge introduces a chain or tx shape not already wired.

If Bridge needs Bitcoin source signing, mirror BitcoinSpokeService.signAndSubmitRawTransaction exactly: gate on walletMode==='TRADING', manually guard rawTx.data + rawTx.from (Bitcoin API raw tx is NOT schema-validated — AnyRawTxSchema), sign via walletProvider.signTransaction(rawTx.data,false), normalizePsbtToBase64, then radfi.requestRadfiSignature({userAddress,signedBase64Tx,relayData}, accessToken). Require the caller to pass relayData (not recoverable from rawTx).

Author a Bridge frontend dispatcher mirroring apps/demo/src/components/swaps-api/lib/signAndBroadcast.ts: export signAndBroadcast<Feature>Tx({chainKey,tx,walletProvider}), is<Feature>SignableChain(chainKey), waitForTxFinality(chainKey,walletProvider,txHash); switch on getXChainType, narrow tx per-chain, throw a typed <Feature>SignError('sdk-gap'/'malformed-payload') for unsupported chains, and route Bitcoin to the spoke-service path (Bitcoin excluded from the wallet-only switch).

If a Bridge API HTTP client is introduced, mirror SwapsApiService: one method per endpoint returning Promise<Result<T>> (never throw), valibot-validate every response, optional trailing RequestOverrideConfig per call, errors tagged feature:'backend' + context.endpoint. Expose it on the Sodax facade (e.g. sodax.api.bridge) and add a CustomApiConfig slice if it can live at its own host.

If Bridge adopts a backend submit-tx flow, replicate the SwapService pattern: add a BridgeClientOptions { useBackendSubmitTx?: boolean } under SodaxOptionalConfig.bridgeOptions (client-side runtime toggle, default false, distinct from the data `bridge` slot), thread useBackendSubmitTx into BridgeService, and branch bridge() into a backend 2-step submitTx() (call api.bridge.submitTx + poll getSubmitTxStatus until 'executed') with a fallbackBridgeSteps() client-relay path on any non-success; split the deadline (reserve ~1/3 capped at 20s) so total wall-clock never exceeds one timeout. Add matching dapp-kit hooks useBridgeApiSubmitTx (mutation, retry:3) + useBridgeApiSubmitTxStatus (query, requires txHash+srcChainKey, refetchInterval 1000ms stop on executed/failed).

Create packages/skills/skills/sodax-sdk/bridge-api/SKILL.md as a SINGLE granular SKILL.md (no own knowledge subtree): YAML frontmatter {name: sodax-sdk-bridge-api, description: '<class> <access path> <endpoint count>, Use when … , Skill links into the parent sodax-sdk knowledge tree.'}; body sections H1 title → intro (access/Result-contract/error-tags) → blockquote to higher-level orchestrator → '## Step 1 — Clarify with user before coding' → '## Integration workflow' (ai-rules.md FIRST, then the new feature md, then siblings, then result-and-errors/error-codes) → feature anti-patterns → '## Migration workflow (v1 → v2)' → '## Verification' (incl. pnpm tsc --noEmit + Result branch checks) → '## Related granular skills'.

Create packages/skills/skills/sodax-sdk/integration/knowledge/features/bridge-api.md following the swaps-api.md anatomy: H1 '# Bridge API — `BridgeApiService`' → intro contract → '## Methods' (single ts block, one signature per endpoint grouped + commented) → '## Wire shapes (bigint vs decimal)' → '## Common call shapes' → '## Status fields' table → '## Per-call overrides' → '## Custom endpoint' → '## Error handling' → '## Cross-references'. (Note the existing integration/knowledge/features/bridge.md is the on-chain BridgeService doc — the API doc is a separate file.)

Create packages/sdk/docs/BRIDGE_API.md mirroring docs/SWAPS_API.md structure (title, Methods ts block, Wire shapes, Examples, Status fields, Configuration with ApiConfig/CustomApiConfig, Result<T>/Error Handling, See also). docs/BRIDGE.md already exists for the on-chain service — keep them distinct and cross-link. Add a cross-link line into BRIDGE.md/BACKEND_API.md and run pnpm check:ai.

Register the new skill in 3 places: (1) parent sodax-sdk/SKILL.md — add a routing-table row and append bridge-api.md to the step-3 features list; (2) packages/skills/AGENTS.md — append sodax-sdk/bridge-api to the package table, the consumer-situation table, and the directory-tree comment; (3) re-use existing migration feature docs unless Bridge needs its own. Add 'backend' is already a SodaxFeature so no new error feature tag is needed for an HTTP-client-layer Bridge API.

Add tests mirroring the PR: <Chain>WalletProvider.test.ts for any new signAndSendTransaction (the PR added +68 Solana / +61 Stacks / +118 Stellar test lines and +64 BitcoinSpokeService test lines), and SwapsApiService.test.ts-style coverage if a BridgeApiService is added. Update wallet-sdk-core/stellar config tests if a new sorobanRpcUrl-like field is introduced.
